speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Go, go, my servant, take thou Troilus’ horse;

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Present the fair steed to my Lady Cressid.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Fellow, commend my service to her beauty.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Tell her I have chastised the amorous Trojan

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: And am her knight by proof.

speaker: MAN
text: I go, my lord.

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Renew, renew! The fierce Polydamas

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Hath beat down Menon; bastard Margareton

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Hath Doreus prisoner,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: And stands colossus-wise, waving his beam

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Upon the pashèd corses of the kings

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Epistrophus and Cedius. Polyxenes is slain,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Amphimachus and Thoas deadly hurt,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Patroclus ta’en or slain, and Palamedes

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Sore hurt and bruised. The dreadful Sagittary

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: Appals our numbers. Haste we, Diomed,

speaker: AGAMEMNON
text: To reinforcement, or we perish all.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Go, bear Patroclus’ body to Achilles,

speaker: NESTOR
text: And bid the snail-paced Ajax arm for shame.

speaker: NESTOR
text: There is a thousand Hectors in the field.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Now here he fights on Galathe his horse,

speaker: NESTOR
text: And here lacks work; anon he’s there afoot

speaker: NESTOR
text: And there they fly or die, like scalèd schools

speaker: NESTOR
text: Before the belching whale; then is he yonder,

speaker: NESTOR
text: And there the strawy Greeks, ripe for his edge,

speaker: NESTOR
text: Fall down before him like a mower’s swath.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Here, there, and everywhere he leaves and takes,

speaker: NESTOR
text: Dexterity so obeying appetite

speaker: NESTOR
text: That what he will he does, and does so much

speaker: NESTOR
text: That proof is called impossibility.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: O, courage, courage, princes! Great Achilles

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Is arming, weeping, cursing, vowing vengeance.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Patroclus’ wounds have roused his drowsy blood,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Together with his mangled Myrmidons,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That noseless, handless, hacked and chipped, come to him,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Crying on Hector. Ajax hath lost a friend

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And foams at mouth, and he is armed and at it,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Roaring for Troilus, who hath done today

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Mad and fantastic execution,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Engaging and redeeming of himself

speaker: ULYSSES
text: With such a careless force and forceless care

speaker: ULYSSES
text: As if that luck, in very spite of cunning,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Bade him win all.

speaker: AJAX
text: Troilus, thou coward Troilus!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Ay, there, there!

speaker: NESTOR
text: So, so, we draw together.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Where is this Hector?—

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Come, come, thou boy-queller, show thy face!

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Know what it is to meet Achilles angry.

speaker: ACHILLES
text: Hector! Where’s Hector? I will none but Hector.
